rulings.Case Title: PETER ENGLERT VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On The Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 27, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER PETER ENGLERT's Hearing On The Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate Petitioner Peter Englert's petition for writ of administrative mandate is denied. Per federal law, all nonconflicting California traffic laws are applicable in federal enclaves including the Presidio. There is no federal law applicable to federal enclaves that conflicts with California's law authorizing the DMV's administrative suspension of Mr. Englert's driver's license. The fact that Mr. Englert was cited and may be prosecuted for violating a federal regulation rather than a California statute prohibiting driving while intoxicated or driving with an excessive blood alcohol level does not preclude DMV's ability to apply California administrative per se procedure to Mr. Englert. Neither due process nor any other federal right of Mr. Englert is violated by the DMV's administrative suspension of his license. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Department of Motor Vehicles is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: BENJAMIN PESSAH VS. CHRISTIAN PINEIRO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Christian Pineiro, Vadim Ananyev, Arash Ghanadan Motion For Order Compelling Complete Response Of Jay Chen, Person Most Qualified For Defendant Beyond Group, Llc., To Deposition Question; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Beyond Group, Llc Attorneys Bledsoe Diestel Treppa Crane, Llp (For Tentative Ruling Purposes Only Part 2 Of 2)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 27, 2018, Line 1, Defendants Christian Pineiro, Vadim Ananyev, Arash Ghanadan's Motion For Order Compelling Complete Response Of Jay Chen, Person Most Qualified For Defendant Beyond Group, Llc., To Deposition Question; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Beyond Group, Llc Attorneys Bledsoe Diestel Treppa Crane, Llp (For Tentative Ruling Purposes Only Part 2 Of 2) Counsel for plaintiff was entitled to testimony by the witness establishing the foundation for the assertion of privilege, including the subject matter of the conversation (which, by itself, would not disclose privileged information) and whether there were any parts of the conversation that were not privileged (i.e., that did not pass along a privileged communication by an attorney for defendant, or was not made for the purpose of collecting information at the request of the attorney for defendant to enable the attorney to render legal services to defendant, such as preparing discovery responses). Plaintiff's motion to compel therefore is GRANTED to the extent that (1) the witness must answer questions reasonably designed to elicit the necessary foundation for assertion of the privilege and (2) the witness must answer questions about non-privileged parts of the conversation. To the extent that the foundation is established, assertion of the privilege must be respected. To the extent that the foundation is not established, or that only part of the conversation fell within the privilege, the witness must disclose the non-privileged part of the conversation. Because the deposition record does not show a lack of substantial justification in the positions taken by either party to this dispute, the parties' cross-requests for sanctions are DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jeffwohl@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (end of part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BENJAMIN PESSAH VS. CHRISTIAN PINEIRO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Compelling Complete Response Of Jay Chen, Person Most Qualified For Defendant Beyond Group, Llc., To Deposition Question; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Beyond Group, Llc Attorneys Bledsoe Diestel Treppa Crane, Llp
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 27, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANTS CHRISTIAN PINEIRO, VADIM ANANYEV, ARASH GHANADAN'S Motion For Order Compelling Complete Response Of Jay Chen, Person Most Qualified For Defendant Beyond Group, Llc., To Deposition Question; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Beyond Group, Llc Attorneys Bledsoe Diestel Treppa Crane, Llp (Part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Jeff Wohl, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Benjamin Pessah's motion to compel is GRANTED in part, but the parties' cross-requests for sanctions are DENIED. A communication between two agents of an organization that conveys a privileged communication by an attorney for the organization, or that is made for the purpose of collecting information to enable the attorney to render legal services to the organization, is privileged from discovery, even if the attorney is not a party to the communication. Cal. Evid. Code § 952 ("As used in this article, 'confidential communication between client and lawyer' means information transmitted between a client and his or her lawyer in the course of that relationship and in confidence by a means which, so far as the client is aware, discloses the information to no third persons other than those who are present to further the interest of the client in the consultation or those to whom disclosure is reasonably necessary for the transmission of the information or the accomplishment of the purpose for which the lawyer is consulted, and includes a legal opinion formed and the advice given by the lawyer in the course of that relationship." (emphasis supplied); Zurich American Ins. Co. v. Superior Court, 155 Cal. App. 4th 1485 (2007). Here, Jay Chen, who was designated as the person most qualified to testify on behalf of defendant Beyond Group, LLC, was instructed at deposition not to disclose the content of a conversation he had with Claudia Yu, identified as an agent of defendant, approximately a year after the shooting incident that gave rise to the lawsuit. Although counsel for defendant represented that the conversation involved "[d]iscussing responses to discovery in connection with the request from an attorney," Chen Depo., 54:6-7, counsel was not testifying under oath, and no foundation was laid by the witness himself to support invocation of the privilege. Indeed, when plaintiff's counsel asked the witness what was the purpose of the conversation, counsel for defendant objected and instructed the witness not to answer the question. Id. at 54:12-24. (End of part 1 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NEDRA GRUBBS-FERGUSON VS. SAFEWAY INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Safeway Inc.S Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 27, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Defendant Safeway Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first cause of action for premises liability and granted as to the second and third causes of action for gross negligence and negligence per se. Based on the video and the other evidence presented by Ms. Grubbs-Ferguson, there is a triable dispute whether Safeway had constructive knowledge of the spilled liquid. (Ortega v. Kmart Corp. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 1200, 1212 ("plaintiffs may demonstrate the storekeeper had constructive notice of the dangerous condition if they can show that the site had not been inspected within a reasonable period of time so that a person exercising due care would have discovered and corrected the hazard.")). Safeway is entitled to summary adjudication of the negligence per se cause of action because Ms. Grubbs-Ferguson failed to identify a statute which protected a class of persons including her from the type of harm which she suffered and whose violation was the cause of her injury. (Evidence Code 669). Safeway is entitled to a summary adjudication of the cause of action for gross negligence because Ms. Grubbs-Ferguson has failed to create a triable issue whether Safeway acted in an extreme departure from the standard of care or exhibited want of even scant care. Safeway was performing inspections at regular intervals and hired an employee whose only duty was to perform such inspections. Evidence that employees did not sign-in in person but had to log in on a computer, prior lawsuits against Safeway within the last five years, and Mr. Rose's declaration that he has often seen dirty floors and sometimes they are not cleaned for 30 minutes does not show an extreme departure from the standard of care. (Grebing v. 24 Hour Fitness USA, Inc. (2015) 234 Cal.App.4th 631, 639 (based on undisputed evidence that defendant "took several measures to ensure that its exercise equipment and facility were well maintained," the court found that "[defendant's conduct] cannot reasonably be regarded as demonstrating a want of even scant care or an extreme departure from the ordinary standard of conduct.")). Plaintiff has not shown how further discovery would reveal facts to support her negligence per se and gross negligence causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Grubbs-Ferguson is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFFS' ASSOCIATION, ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 27, 2018, Line 10, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION Respondent City and County of San Francisco's motion for reconsideration of the September 26, 2018 order granting petition for enforcement of arbitration award is treated as an invitation to reconsider the September 26 order per Le Francois without compliance with the requirements of CCP 1008 and, as so treated, is granted. The September 26 order is vacated and a new order will be entered denying the petition for enforcement of the arbitration award. The MOU unambiguously provides that review of a Sheriff's disciplinary decision that results in a decision by the reviewer to impose lesser discipline is not binding on the Sheriff. Because the MOU has the force and effect of an ordinance, the parties' agreement for binding arbitration which conflicted with the MOU is not enforceable per Boren and its progeny. Nor are past practices, waiver or estoppel grounds to depart from the plain language of the MOU. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
